verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a p24 family transmembrane trafficking protein (p24α2). These proteins are soluble and membrane-associated components of COPII and COPI-coated vesicles in the secretory pathway, localizing to the ER and Golgi apparatus. TMED9 is a cargo and coat protein, not a plasma membrane receptor. No isoform is anchored at the cell surface; it cycles between ER-Golgi compartments. Cell-state induction: p24 proteins are constitutive ER-Golgi residents, no evidence for surface display under stress or activation. Tissue restriction: ubiquitously expressed ER-Golgi protein, not germline or developmental-lineage restricted. Lysosomal exocytosis: no documented exocytosis-based surface trafficking. Dual localization: no PM pool; strictly endomembrane. Stable surface attachment: not applicable—no secreted or TM precursor isoform with surface anchoring. No clinical or experimental evidence of anti-TMED9 surface targeting or PM engagement.

- Previous symbols: (none)
- HGNC gene-group memberships: Transmembrane p24 trafficking proteins
- CD nomenclature: (none assigned)
- NCBI summary: This gene is a member of a family of genes encoding transport proteins located in the endoplasmic reticulum and the Golgi. A similar gene in mouse is the target of microRNA miR-296, which is part of an imprinted cluster. [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2016]
